name: Mogwai
description: Minimal, channel/stream-based view library with no macros

description.value: A minimal Rust view library for building browser GUIs with channels/streams instead of callbacks and no virtual DOM (MOGWAI = minimal, obvious, graphical, widget application interface)
description.comment: README: 'mogwai is a Rust crate for building GUI applications, primarily in the browser'; events are modeled as futures/streams rather than callbacks. Note: the candidate tagline 'no macros' is now dated — mogwai 0.7 ships an rsx! macro (views can still be built with plain Rust functions).

license.comment: MIT only (Cargo.toml 'license = "MIT"'), not the MIT/Apache-2.0 dual license common to most Rust web frameworks.

documentation.comment: docs.rs API docs plus an in-crate 'an_introduction' guide module; a separate 'Cooking with Mogwai' cookbook is linked from the README (zyghost.com/guides/mogwai-cookbook).

latest-stable-version.comment: Latest crates.io release as of 2026-07-19; still pre-1.0 (0.7 is a from-scratch rewrite with a cross-platform View trait).

maintenance-status.comment: 0.7.0 through 0.7.6 released across 2025-06 to 2026-07; repo last pushed 2026-07-14. Development is active but effectively solo (Schell Scivally).

maturity.comment: Pre-1.0 (0.7.x) and API is actively changing — 0.7 is a recent ground-up rewrite (edition 2024, new cross-platform View trait). No widely documented production users, so rated experimental over beta despite the project existing since 2019.

corporate-foundation-backing.comment: Solo/community project by Schell Scivally; funded via GitHub Sponsors, no corporate or foundation backing.

reactivity-model.comment: Does not fit the tag set neatly: mogwai is fundamentally channel/stream/futures-based — view events flow into async logic loops as messages ('channels instead of callbacks', unidirectional, Elm-adjacent), while reactive Proxy values patch the exact bound DOM node with no diffing (signal-like fine-grained updates). Tagged elm+signals with this caveat; it is neither a classic Model/Msg/update Elm loop nor an auto-tracked signals graph.

component-model.comment: 0.7 widgets are Rust structs (e.g. #[derive(ViewChild)] on a struct holding elements/listeners/proxies) with impl blocks driving an async step loop. Views can also be assembled ad hoc inside plain functions via rsx!.

view-authoring.comment: Primary authoring is the rsx! macro (JSX-like Rust markup); README concepts also state views 'may be constructed and nested using plain Rust functions', i.e. a builder-style API without the macro.

fine-grained-updates.comment: A Proxy value, when modified, replaces/patches exactly the node it is bound to; 'DOM mutation is explicit ... no performance overhead from vdom diffing'.

uses-virtual-dom.comment: Explicitly no virtual DOM; the 'why' section contrasts mogwai with vdom-and-diff frameworks.

async-await-in-components.comment: Events are futures; widget logic is written as async loops that .await event streams (e.g. self.on_click.next().await), spawned via wasm_bindgen_futures::spawn_local / mogwai::spawn.

global-state-solution.comment: No built-in context/provide-inject or global store; shared state is passed explicitly via channels (senders/receivers) and Proxy values.

compiles-to.comment: Browser target compiles to WebAssembly via the 'web' feature (wasm-bindgen/web-sys). Also has an 'ssr' backend for server-side string rendering, but the client runs as WASM.

primary-build-tool.comment: Getting-started uses Trunk (trunk serve); projects are scaffolded from mogwai-template via cargo-generate.

official-cli.comment: No first-party build/dev-server CLI; relies on Trunk plus a cargo-generate starter template.

dev-server-hmr.comment: Dev loop via Trunk, which auto-rebuilds and live-reloads the browser; no view/RSX hot reload.

minimal-wasm-bundle-size.comment: No documented, reproducible minimal hello-world .wasm measurement under a consistent opt profile; mogwai advertises being 'small and fast' but publishes no methodical number, so omitted per RESEARCH.md rather than guessing.

js-interop.comment: Built on wasm-bindgen and web-sys (mogwai 'addresses challenges with web-sys'); element/event wrappers surface web-sys underneath.

js-interop-ergonomics.comment: Standard wasm-bindgen/web-sys interop with the usual boilerplate; mogwai wraps common element/style/event needs but dropping to raw web APIs is unremarkable.

no-std-minimal-runtime.comment: Depends on std, web-sys and async runtimes; no no_std mode.

client-side-rendering-spa.comment: Primary target is client-rendered browser GUIs / SPAs (see the spa-routing example).

server-side-rendering.comment: 'ssr' is a default feature (ssr.rs module); README lists server-side rendering as a focus. Cross-platform View trait lets the same view render to an SSR string backend.

static-site-generation.comment: SSR can render views to HTML strings, but there is no built-in static-site-generation pipeline (route pre-rendering at build time).

hydration.comment: 0.7's crates are mogwai, mogwai-macros, plus benchmark/xtask helpers — the earlier standalone mogwai-hydrator is not part of the 0.7 rewrite, and no client hydration of server HTML is documented. SSR renders strings; hydration marked none pending official support.

streaming-ssr.comment: SSR renders to a complete HTML string; no documented streaming SSR.

backend-integration.comment: Mogwai is a view library, not a full-stack framework — it has no first-party server integration. SSR emits HTML strings usable behind any server, but no specific backend (Axum/Actix/etc.) is integrated, so left null rather than asserting a full-stack mode.

isomorphic-routing.comment: Mogwai ships no router at all; routing is hand-rolled (see below), so there is no shared server/client router.

official-router.comment: No first-party router crate/module. The spa-routing example implements routing manually with a user-defined Route enum, TryFrom parsing, and a HashChangeEvent listener.

router-name.comment: No router crate; the example shows users writing their own hash-based routing.

nested-routes.comment: No built-in routing, hence no nested-route construct; any nesting is manual match logic.

dynamic-route-params.comment: The framework provides no route-parameter feature. The example parses params (e.g. profile/{username}) by hand-splitting the hash, but that is user code, not a mogwai capability.

typed-routes.comment: No framework-provided typed routing (no derive/enum route system). The example's Route enum is ordinary user code with manual string parsing.

lazy-code-split-routes.comment: No per-route code splitting / lazy loading (no router).

styling-approach.comment: Inline style attributes in rsx! (e.g. style:cursor = "pointer") plus ordinary external CSS files; no scoped-CSS, CSS-in-Rust, or first-class Tailwind integration.

scoped-styles.comment: No built-in component-scoped styling; styles are global CSS or inline attributes.

component-library-ecosystem.comment: Essentially no ready-made UI component-kit crates; users build widgets themselves.

forms-inputs.comment: HtmlInputElement is supported; inputs are wired via event channels and Proxy values (controlled). No dedicated form-helper or two-way-binding abstraction.

svg-canvas-support.comment: web-sys 'SvgElement' feature is enabled and the repo ships a dedicated svg example; canvas can be reached through web-sys interop.

suspense-loading-ui.comment: No declarative suspense/loading-boundary primitive; async loading states are handled manually via streams/proxies.

documentation-quality.comment: docs.rs API docs, an in-crate 'an_introduction' guide, a separate 'Cooking with Mogwai' cookbook, and 6 repo examples — adequate but not an extensive, fully up-to-date tutorial series (README example still shows older builder! usage in places).

api-ergonomics.comment: rsx! is concise, but the stream/channel model plus struct-widget + async step-loop pattern carries a distinct learning curve and some boilerplate relative to signal-first peers.

compile-times.comment: Small dependency tree, but rsx! is a proc-macro; no published benchmark, so rated moderate conservatively.

examples-count.comment: 6 example directories on main (counter, list-of-gizmos, nested-components, spa-routing, svg, todomvc) — in the 3-10 'limited' band.

interactive-playground.comment: No hosted online playground/REPL found; there is a cookbook guide and repo examples, but no interactive sandbox.

browser-devtools.comment: No dedicated devtools/inspector beyond generic browser tools.

testing-support.comment: wasm-bindgen-test is a dev-dependency for browser tests; examples also include plain #[test] unit tests (e.g. route parsing). No dedicated component-testing harness.

crates-io-downloads.comment: All-time downloads of the mogwai crate as of 2026-07-19.

contributors.comment: GitHub contributors API count on 2026-07-19; heavily dominated by the maintainer (schell ~270 commits, others in single digits).

community-size.comment: Single primary maintainer with a small Matrix/Element chat room; ~6 contributors and 428 stars. Effectively solo.

ecosystem-crates.comment: Only in-tree helpers (mogwai-macros) plus a starter template; no third-party routers or component kits.

notable-production-users.comment: No widely documented production/company users found.

meta-framework-full-stack-kit.comment: No associated full-stack meta-framework; mogwai-template is a project starter, not a full-stack layer.
